content:
# Completion Vote
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Vote: Complete

## Evidence

- Primary stage artifact persistence is wired correctly: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4647-4655` builds `RecordProducer::Agent` from `envelope.metadata.backend_used.family` and `envelope.metadata.model_used.model_id`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:4704-4712` stores that producer on the persisted `ArtifactRecord`.
- Supporting artifact persistence paths also use `InvocationEnvelope` metadata rather than configured targets in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/completion.rs:138-140`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:111-113` and `170-172`, and `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:239-241`, `351-353`, `443-445`, and `559-561`.
- `ArtifactRecord` carries producer metadata in `src/contexts/project_run_record/model.rs:328-344`, and the filesystem adapter writes the full artifact struct unchanged with `serde_json::to_string_pretty(artifact)` in `src/adapters/fs.rs:1105-1106`, so `backend_family` and `model_id` reach `history/artifacts/*.json`.
- `AgentExecutionService` preserves adapter-reported backend/model metadata before persistence in `src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:219-224`, and the unit test at `src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:401-437` asserts divergent adapter metadata survives invocation.
- There is end-to-end regression coverage for persisted artifacts using envelope metadata instead of configured targets in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs:1513-1605`.

## Remaining Work

None.
